title: Daily Briefing — Apr 21
headline: Tech and cyclicals lead, greed surges — time to tighten stops and respect the data.
body: Right, so let's look at what the flows are telling us. SPY and QQQ both tagging new highs, handily above their 20 and 50 MAs (SPY: $708.72 vs 20MA $669.62), but Fear & Greed just printed 70.9 — that's a sharp ramp from 15.6 a month back. We're not at full-blown euphoria yet, but greed regimes don't last forever, so start dialing in on risk.

Sector rotation is clean: Tech (XLK $154.56), Industrials (XLI $173.90), Utilities (XLU $45.75), and Real Estate (XLRE $44.64) are all in the leading quadrant with price momentum above their short-term MAs. Meanwhile, Discretionary, Staples, Healthcare, and Communication are lagging. Translation: Defensive plays are underperforming, risk-on leadership is dominant — for now.

Contrarian red flag: Retail is piling into momentum (look at DELL + ARM + Meta runners), while the put/call is nowhere near max fear, and F&G is screaming greed. No active sell signal — but when everyone wants tech at ATH, that's usually when mean reversion shows up. If it's in the press, it's in the price. Patience, react, don't predict.
callout_body:
SPY: Watch the 20MA at $669.62 for trend guardrail
QQQ: Mean reversion zone is $601
XLK: $140.45 (20MA) is your line in the sand
XLF: $50.36 (20MA) — financials holding, so no 'canary' risk
VIX staying below 20 — calm, but don't get lulled
action_items: Raise stops on tech leaders approaching parabolic extensions (AAPL $273, NVDA $202, Meta $670 — all stretched above 20MA).
Don't chase breakouts in laggards; look at Industrials (XLI $173.90, 20MA $166.86) and Real Estate (XLRE $44.64, triggered) for rotational follow-through if market holds.
closing_quote: Less trades = more wins. Abundance mindset. If you missed the move, wait for the mean.
